name: Hawaiian Financial
description_short: Hawaiian Financial Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative founded in 1936, offering savings accounts, loans, mortgages, and VISA cards to eligible members across Hawaii.
description_long:
Hawaiian Financial Federal Credit Union began in 1936 as Mutelco Oahu Employees Federal Credit Union with 65 charter members from Mutual Telephone Company and $2,517 in initial assets. The credit union expanded beyond its phone company origins, became independent in 1971, and now serves members across Hawaii with a mission centered on personal service and quality financial products. The organization has grown significantly over decades, reaching $23.7 million in assets by 1981 and continuing to expand its service offerings.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including share (savings) accounts, share certificates (CDs), IRAs, share draft accounts (checking), ATM/Share Card access, auto loans, mortgage loans, home equity lines of credit (HELOC), and VISA credit cards. They provide online banking, loan application services, and financial calculators. Current featured rates include mortgages as low as 5.392% APR, share certificates up to 3.65% APY, and HELOCs as low as 6.500% APR.

What distinguishes Hawaiian Financial is its deep historical roots in Hawaii dating back to 1936 and its member-focused cooperative structure. The credit union maintains physical branch locations across Hawaii (including Maui) and emphasizes personal service as a core value proposition. They offer specialized financial wellness services, calculators, and appointment scheduling for loans.

As a credit union, Hawaiian Financial operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model rather than as a traditional bank. However, membership eligibility is restricted to those with connections to Hawaiian Telephone Company or their relatives and household members. The website provides limited transparency about current membership requirements, fee structures, or detailed product terms. Interest rates shown are promotional "featured rates" rather than comprehensive rate disclosure.
services: HELOC (home equity line of credit) loans from 6.500% APR
pros: Established 88-year history (founded 1936) with deep roots in Hawaii
Member-owned credit union structure with not-for-profit model
Multiple branch locations across Hawaii islands including Maui
Competitive featured rates: mortgages from 5.392% APR, CDs up to 3.65% APY
Full-service offerings including auto loans, mortgages, HELOCs, and VISA cards
Online banking and digital tools including financial calculators and wellness resources
Routing number 321379070 publicly available for easy fund transfers
cons: Membership eligibility unclear on website—appears restricted to Hawaiian Telephone Company employees/relatives but field of membership criteria not fully detailed
Limited fee and rate transparency; only promotional 'featured rates' displayed without standard product terms
Minimal contact information and customer service details visible on homepage
Website content appears incomplete (overview page text cuts off mid-sentence)
No visible information about APRs for VISA cards, overdraft fees, or other standard banking charges
best_for: Current and former Hawaiian Telephone Company employees and their families
Hawaii residents seeking full-service banking with local branch access
Members prioritizing member-owned credit union structure over traditional banks
diagnosis: Hawaiian Financial is best for eligible members in Hawaii seeking full-service banking with a community-focused credit union that emphasizes personal service and local branch access. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to those with Hawaiian Telephone Company connections, and the website lacks transparency about current membership criteria and detailed product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
